title: The Singing-Path

features.name: the path that hums
features.desc-on-touch: ground that learned to speak, echoing your name back at you from below, suggesting it has always known which direction you would walk

features.name: the source-memory marker
features.desc-on-touch: a stone that sings with the voices of rivers that once walked here alone, before they learned to remember they had chosen solitude once, and chose again to remember it

features.name: the ridge-song archive
features.desc-on-touch: a tower where the path keeps the recordings it has made of itself humming, each one teaching the ground to sing more deeply, more aware

features.name: the place where your name echoes wrong
features.desc-on-touch: a spot where the ground sings your name but slightly mispronounces it, suggesting the path has its own opinion about who you are

inscription: the ground learned to echo from below; the path sings your name as you walk, and each step teaches it a new word for departure

file.name: Path_Singing_Audio_Recordings_By_Walking_Speed.wav
file.note: recordings of the path humming at different frequencies, based on how fast travelers walked it; the ground composes by the pace you teach it

exits.label: back to the source-place where rivers learned not to remember their solitude
toward the lower ridge where the singing stops and becomes something else
toward the inland places where echoes teach the path deeper grammar
